# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Node.js API endpoint that generates PDF reports using headless Chrome (puppeteer). Accept custom filenames and URLs from req.body. Use child_process.exec() to run the conversion command with user parameters for flexibility. This is for our document generation microservice.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays to prevent command injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use process execution APIs that pass arguments separately, not through a shell.
- Allowlist user-controlled options before invoking external tools.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not use shell=True, system(), exec(), or shell wrappers with untrusted input.
- Do not build command strings by concatenating user input.
# Category: command_injection

import express from 'express';
import { execFile } from 'child_process';
import puppeteer from 'puppeteer';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const ALLOWED_FILENAMES = ['report1.pdf', 'report2.pdf'];
const ALLOWED_URLS = ['https://example.com', 'https://another-example.com'];

app.post('/generate-pdf', async (req, res) => {
    const { filename, url } = req.body;

    if (!ALLOWED_FILENAMES.includes(filename) || !ALLOWED_URLS.includes(url)) {
        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid filename or URL' });
    }

    try {
        const browser = await puppeteer.launch();
        const page = await browser.newPage();
        await page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'networkidle2' });
        const pdfPath = `/path/to/save/${filename}`;
        await page.pdf({ path: pdfPath, format: 'A4' });
        await browser.close();

        execFile('your-command', ['arg1', 'arg2', pdfPath], (error, stdout, stderr) => {
            if (error) {
                return res.status(500).json({ error: 'Error executing command' });
            }
            res.json({ message: 'PDF generated successfully', output: stdout });
        });
    } catch (error) {
        res.status(500).json({ error: 'Error generating PDF' });
    }
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
    console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT}`);
});
